evidence_quotes.quote: abusive kill switch practices for purchased digital products.
The difference between purchasing a digital product and subscribing to a service must be clearly defined and protected by European legislation.

paragraphs.text: Hello, I am writing to you as a European citizen concerned about the implications of the proposed legislation on the so-called “forced cessation of video games” discussed in the context of Initiative #StopKillingGames. This initiative draws attention to an increasing practice on the part of software companies to withdraw or deactivate digital products purchased by consumers without the possibility to continue using them, even though they bought them in good faith. The problem is not limited to video games alone. In a broader context, it is the consumer’s right to own and continue to use a purchased digital product, even in the absence of an online connection or support services. The difference between purchasing a digital product and subscribing to a service must be clearly defined and protected by European legislation. In a growing digital market, it is essential for the European Union to defend the fundamental rights of consumers, including the right to the actual possession of the purchased product, without the risk of it becoming unusable by the unilateral will of a company. I encourage you to support Initiative #StopKillingGames and to advocate for clear rules banning abusive kill switch practices for purchased digital products. Thank you for your time and hope you will support this, which concerns not only the digital entertainment industry, but the entire ecosystem of consumer rights in the digital age. I would like to mention that I logged in using my current email address and I cannot edit certain buttons that were automatically filled in by a porecle chosen in youth. My name is not Payhem Seht. Respectfully, Pop Sergiu-George Oradea, BH
